title: acer weiri-laciniatum
intro: Acer weiri-laciniatum, also known as the paperbark maple, is a deciduous tree native to Japan, characterized by its distinctive papery bark and lobed leaves. The plant has been used in traditional Chinese medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. Acer weiri-laciniatum has been reported to have various health benefits, such as reducing blood pressure and cholesterol levels, as well as exhibiting antibacterial and antiviral properties. In herbal preparations, the bark of the Acer weiri-laciniatum tree is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures to leverage its medicinal properties.
